perturbation.original_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of any amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the undersigned or contained herein and therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned acknowledge that none of them are responsible for the timely filing of any amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the undersigned or contained herein and therein, and shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others.
perturbation.explanation: The original text assigns responsibility to each signatory for their own filings. This change explicitly states that none of the undersigned are responsible for the timely filing of any amendments which introduces an in-text contradiction and creates uncertainty regarding who, if anyone, is legally responsible for ensuring filings are accurate and on time. This contradicts the initial agreement to a joint filing and the purpose of the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing with each of the Reporting Persons (as such term is defined in the Schedule 13D referred to below) on behalf of each of them of a statement on Schedule 13D (including amendments thereto and statements on Schedule 13G) with respect to the Common Stock, par value $0.01 per share of Macy's, Inc., a corporation organized under the laws of the State of Delaware, and that this agreement may be included as an exhibit to such joint filing. The undersigned acknowledge that none of them are responsible for the timely filing of any amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the undersigned or contained herein and therein, and shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this agreement as of May 11, 2020.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that "none of them are responsible for the timely filing of any amendments," directly contradicting the initial purpose and agreement of a joint filing as stated in the provided text. The original text assigns responsibility to each party for their filings and that this agreement may be included as an exhibit to such joint filing. This introduces a structural flaw that makes the agreement unenforceable, aligning with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type definition.

perturbation.original_text: VESA EQUITY INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Marek Spurny Title:Authorized Signatory /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title:Authorized Signatory
perturbation.changed_text: VESA EQUITY INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Unauthorized Person Title:Authorized Signatory /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title:Authorized Signatory
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Marek Spurny' to 'Unauthorized Person' introduces a conflict. Initially, Marek Spurny signed as an authorized signatory on behalf of VESA EQUITY INVESTMENT S.À R.L. Now, the text states he is an 'Unauthorized Person,' while still holding the 'Authorized Signatory' title. This creates ambiguity and undermines the validity of the agreement and contradicting the signature of the authorized signatory Pascal Leclerc, creating doubts about their legitimacy too. It also creates doubt regarding to the legitimacy of the whole document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VESA EQUITY INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Unauthorized Person Title:Authorized Signatory /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title:Authorized Signatory
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by labeling Marek Spurny as an 'Unauthorized Person' while simultaneously retaining the title 'Authorized Signatory'. This creates a clear and irreconcilable conflict, undermining the validity of his signature and creating uncertainty about the legitimacy of the agreement. It directly clashes with the contradicted text, which is the same changed text, because the statement inherently is contradictory. The perturbation explanation is that the modification creates a situation where someone is simultaneously an authorized signatory and unauthorized, which is inherently contradictory and creates doubts about the legitimacy of the entire document.

perturbation.original_text: EP INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Marek Spurny Title:Authorized Signatory /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title:Authorized Signatory
perturbation.changed_text: EP INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Mickey Mouse By: Mickey Mouse Title:Authorized Signatory /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title:Authorized Signatory
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Marek Spurny' to 'Mickey Mouse' introduces a conflict. Initially, Marek Spurny signed as an authorized signatory on behalf of EP INVESTMENT S.À R.L. Now, the text states Mickey Mouse is signing, while still holding the 'Authorized Signatory' title. This is impossible because Mickey Mouse is not human and is an imaginary cartoon. It creates ambiguity and undermines the validity of the agreement and contradicting the signature of the authorized signatory Pascal Leclerc, creating doubts about their legitimacy too. It also creates doubt regarding to the legitimacy of the whole document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EP INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Mickey Mouse By: Mickey Mouse Title:Authorized Signatory /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title:Authorized Signatory
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Marek Spurny' with 'Mickey Mouse' as an authorized signatory of EP INVESTMENT S.À R.L. The contradicted text also states the same i.e 'Mickey Mouse' as an authorized signatory. This change introduces a contradiction because a fictional character cannot legally represent a company as an authorized signatory, therefore creating doubt on the validity of the signature of the other authorized signatory Pascal Leclerc and the legitimacy of the whole document. This directly contradicts the implicit requirement for signatories to be legally competent and authorized to act on behalf of the company, leading to uncertainty about the agreement's enforceability.
